scope: Oxford 3000 / Oxford 5000 licensing/request contour for an English vocabulary course. User-reported OUP permission is accepted as project evidence for oxford_3000_core_a2_part_002_300_v1 final delivery. Final learner-facing US/UK Google Sheets are allowed for this release; ordinary deck-table Postgres import remains disabled.

permission_request.user_intent: User confirmed Oxford is needed for product/market positioning and later reported that OUP allowed all requested uses.
permission_request.next_step: Keep written OUP evidence if received later; do not claim official/endorsed/certified Oxford status unless separately granted.

project_evidence_decision.basis: User reported OUP allowed the requested Oxford 3000/5000 use and explicitly instructed to make this release a Google Sheets delivery.

latest_source_snapshot.selected_scope: Oxford 3000 rows where level_min is one of A2, offset 300, limit 300

source_repairs.reason: The PDF-derived source parser split the light row and promoted the level/POS fragment 'adj. A1,' to a fake headword.

external_terms_snapshot.conclusion: Oxford pages support learner browsing/download for study but do not provide a documented free licence for systematic reuse as a LunaCards production source list. User-reported OUP permission allows local source-package work, but final delivery remains blocked until written evidence or an explicit project evidence decision is stored.

permission_fields_to_request: right to distribute as Google Sheets/workbooks/downloads/app flashcards
whether definitions, examples, pronunciations or audio may be used
allowed_fields: list order/order bands for internal part slicing
Google Sheets/workbook delivery for this release
downloadable/app flashcard reuse for this release
source_policy.attribution_no_endorsement_wording: Oxford 3000 and Oxford 5000 are Oxford University Press word lists. This LunaCards workbook is independently prepared by LunaCards and is not official, certified, approved, or endorsed by Oxford University Press.

row_identity.sense_split_rule: Split one source headword into multiple meaning_id rows when part of speech, sense, or semantic scene differs.

workbook.principle: HSK-style course workbook: one release workbook, source rows, fixed 54 language columns, service sheets for source and QA traceability.
workbook.translation_columns: All spreadsheetCode values from config/language-order.json in exact order.
workbook.example_columns: All spreadsheetCode values from config/language-order.json in exact order.

edition_export_model.principle: Keep one canonical source package and export two buyer-facing English editions through the ordinary LunaCards spreadsheet shell instead of duplicating semantic rows.
edition_export_model.main_sheet_column_contract: 53 buyer-facing word columns -> 53 buyer-facing example columns -> primary English word IPA -> primary English example IPA

editions.deck_title: FlashcardsLuna Oxford 3000 Core A2 Part 002 - US English
FlashcardsLuna Oxford 3000 Core A2 Part 002 - British English

edition_export_model.support_language_word_transcription_poli: out_of_scope_for_oxford_english_learning_edition_workbooks; do not create empty support-language transcription columns

article_policy.english_headword: Oxford-style lemma/headword; do not add artificial a/an/the to English source headwords.
article_policy.support_translation_display: Keep natural articles/gender markers in support-language display where the language normally teaches nouns with them.

edition_export_model.source_package_variant_contract: US/UK pronunciation and spelling variants remain in source/QA artifacts; buyer-facing main sheets show only the primary English edition.

allowed_future_pronunciation_sources: Britfone or documented EN-GB source for EN-GB
other documented pronunciation sources from reference-sources manifest

example_policy.example_source: LunaCards-authored examples with semantic_scene and QA evidence.

user_facing_packaging.principle: Expose two coherent courses plus a complete bundle. Do not expose one giant 5000-row learner workflow and do not expose 19 unrelated products.

courses.title: Oxford 5000 Advanced Extension
courses.scope: Additional Oxford 5000 B2-C1 extension vocabulary beyond Oxford 3000

bundle.title: Complete Oxford 3000 + 5000 Vocabulary
bundle.scope: Bundles Oxford 3000 Core and Oxford 5000 Advanced Extension

default_first_release.scope: first Oxford 3000 Core A1-only source-draft pilot

qa_gate_scope.quality_center: English learner value: learner sense, POS, CEFR fit, US English default, optional EN-GB variant, learner example and allowed source pronunciation.
qa_gate_scope.support_language_role: Support translations/examples help learners understand English in the source-package gate. The US/UK edition workbooks use the final Oxford English-learning sheet contract, not ordinary learn-any-language delivery: support-language word/example cells are present, the opposite English edition is omitted from each buyer-facing main sheet, and empty support-language transcription columns are forbidden.

next_stage_options: Continue the next unfinished Oxford 3000/5000 source package from the source-of-truth docs and contract files.

Apply the standing Oxford auto-publish rule when the next part finishes source-package QA: export final US/UK workbooks, upload native Google Sheets, run readback, update docs/contract and register the gate report.
Keep ordinary deck-table Postgres import and isolated Oxford DB import disabled unless explicitly approved.

final_delivery_decision.scope: Final Google Sheets delivery for oxford_3000_core_a2_part_002_300_v1 US English and British English editions.

article_policy.english_headword: Oxford-style lemma/headword; do not add artificial a/an/the to English source headwords.
article_policy.support_translation_display: Keep natural articles/gender markers in support-language display where the language normally teaches nouns with them.

source_repairs.reason: The PDF-derived source parser split the light row and promoted the level/POS fragment 'adj. A1,' to a fake headword.

latest_edition_pronunciations.pronunciation_source: Britfone with exact-token lookup, documented inflection/initialism fallback and component-backed compound fallback

latest_weak_language_targeted_review.review_scope: Deterministic targeted review of Oxford A1 support-language artifacts. This is source-package QA and not external native-speaker approval.

latest_support_translation_sample_review.review_method: Deterministic Codex semantic plausibility sample review of display translations and example sentences against the reviewed English meaning notes. This is not native-speaker certification and does not replace full final linguistic audit.

latest_support_translation_source_backed.audit_scope: Oxford A1 support-language display translations checked against local indexed source candidates. Sources are candidate evidence, not automatic truth or native-speaker approval.
latest_support_translation_source_backed.does_not_replace: native-speaker certification or full final linguistic audit

latest_support_example_quality_audit.audit_scope: Oxford A1 support-language examples checked with deterministic shape, script, casing, spacing, known naturalness, pedagogical and lexical-anchor gates. This is not native-speaker certification.
latest_support_example_quality_audit.does_not_replace: native-speaker certification or full final linguistic audit

latest_support_article_display_repair.policy: English headwords stay Oxford-style lemmas. Support-language noun displays use natural articles/gender markers for article languages; zero-change checks are valid when batches already include the article display layer.
latest_support_article_display_repair.does_not_replace: native-speaker certification or full final linguistic audit

latest_all_fields_sample_review.does_not_replace: native-speaker certification, final workbook readback or final linguistic audit

latest_edition_exports.source_package_variant_contract: US/UK pronunciation and spelling variants remain in source/QA artifacts; buyer-facing main sheets show only the primary English edition.
